title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Jaundice
intro: Herbal teas for Jaundice are natural remedies made from various herbs and plants that help alleviate the symptoms associated with this liver disorder. They work by stimulating liver function, improving bile flow, and reducing inflammation, which can help to break down bilirubin and relieve jaundice symptoms. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat jaundice include Dandelion root tea, which is known for its ability to stimulate liver function and improve bile flow; Milk Thistle tea, which contains silymarin that helps protect the liver and promote its function; Turmeric tea, which contains curcumin that has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce liver inflammation; and Ginger tea, which helps to stimulate digestion and improve bile flow; and Artichoke tea, which is known for its ability to stimulate bile production and improve liver function. By incorporating these herbal teas into a treatment plan, individuals may be able to alleviate the symptoms of jaundice and support overall liver health.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been traditionally used to treat jaundice due to its hepatoprotective properties. The sesquiterpene lactones, particularly silymarin, in these teas help to treat jaundice by supporting liver function, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting the regeneration of liver cells. The flavonoids and polyphenols present in Silybum marianum teas also aid in the treatment of jaundice by enhancing bile flow and reducing inflammation in the liver. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat jaundice include reduced liver damage, improved liver function, and enhanced overall health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Silybum marianum flowers.
Measure 8 ounces of boiling water and pour it over the flowers.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to mix.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1/4 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Frontiers in pharmacology", Silybum marianum teas for jaundice have been found to contain silybin, a biologically active ingredient that leads to bilirubin reduction and normalization of relevant serum enzymes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat jaundice, but its use may cause side effects such as diarrhea, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Silybum marianum teas, including avoiding its use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with kidney or liver problems, as it may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Silybum marianum tea for jaundice treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea, 3 times a day, for 7-10 days.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Silybum marianum per cup of boiling water.
Increase the dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup after 3-5 days, if needed.
Monitor liver function and bilirubin levels to adjust the treatment plan.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat jaundice due to their hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, are responsible for their therapeutic effects by promoting liver health, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the immune system. By reducing liver inflammation and improving liver function, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help to treat jaundice by promoting the removal of bilirubin and other waste products from the body. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat jaundice include reduced risk of liver damage, improved liver function, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a valuable herbal remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. If using fresh, clean and dry them.
Crush 1-2 tablespoons of the roots to release their flavor and properties.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the crushed roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the roots. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from jaundice symptoms. Consult a doctor before using this remedy.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for jaundice have been scientifically shown to have hepatoprotective properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used as a complementary treatment for jaundice, but it may cause side effects such as hypertension, hypokalemia, and edema due to its licorice root content's ability to inhibit 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, thereby affecting cortisol and aldosterone levels. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it is recommended to limit the consumption to short-term periods and monitor blood pressure and potassium levels closely, as well as avoid consuming it with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for jaundice treatment.
Drink 2 cups of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, 3 times a day, with meals.
Use 2 tablespoons of dried root in 1 cup of boiling water for each tea.
Continue treatment for 7-10 days, under doctor's supervision and guidance.
Monitor liver enzyme levels and bilirubin levels during treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat jaundice, an ailment characterized by a yellowing of the skin and eyes due to elevated bilirubin levels. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Curcuma longa teas, attributed to its bioactive constituents such as curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, help to reduce liver inflammation and improve bile flow, thus aiding in the treatment of jaundice. The curcuminoids in Curcuma longa teas have been shown to exhibit hepatoprotective effects, which help to protect the liver from damage and promote its recovery. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat jaundice include reduced liver inflammation, improved liver function, and faster recovery times, making it a valuable natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Curcuma longa root, 1 cup of water, and a strainer.
Boil the water in a saucepan.
Add the dried Curcuma longa root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture using a strainer.
Drink 1 cup of the Curcuma longa tea, 2-3 times a day, for relief from jaundice symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating jaundice, but its usage may lead to side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and increased risk of bleeding due to its blood-thinning properties. Precautions to take when using Curcuma longa teas include starting with small doses, monitoring liver function, and avoiding it in individuals with bleeding disorders or those taking anticoagulant medications.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Curcuma longa tea for jaundice treatment.
Take 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder in 1 cup of warm water.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, for 3-4 days as directed by doctor.
Monitor liver function and bilirubin levels regularly during treatment.
Follow up with doctor to assess treatment progress and adjust dosage if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaundice, a condition characterized by yellowing of the skin and eyes due to liver dysfunction. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to possess hepatoprotective properties, which help to protect and regenerate liver cells, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms. The diuretic and choleretic properties of Taraxacum officinale teas help to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the removal of bilirubin from the body and reduces jaundice severity. By promoting liver function and stimulating bile production, Taraxacum officinale teas provide a natural and effective treatment for jaundice, offering benefits such as improved liver health and reduced risk of liver damage.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the gathered plant material and place it in a cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup with the plant material.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks, as needed for jaundice treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat jaundice, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, including avoiding excessive consumption, monitoring liver function, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Taraxacum officinale tea for jaundice treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale tea 3 times a day for 7-10 days.
Start with a low dosage of 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2-3 teaspoons per cup if symptoms persist after 7 days.
Monitor liver function and adjust treatment plan as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaundice due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, present in ginger tea help to reduce liver inflammation and promote bile flow, thereby aiding in the treatment of jaundice. The hepatoprotective properties of ginger tea help to protect the liver from damage and promote its natural detoxification processes, contributing to the alleviation of jaundice symptoms. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat jaundice include reduced liver inflammation, improved bile flow, and a natural and non-invasive approach to managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) roots and wash them in cold water.
Peel the ginger roots and chop them into small pieces.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and add 2-3 tablespoons of chopped ginger.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat jaundice, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. To minimize risks, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming Zingiber officinale teas with other medications, and be cautious when using it with people who have bleeding disorders or are prone to bleeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times daily
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginger per cup of boiling water
Take 300-400 mg of ginger extract 2-3 times daily
Consume for 7-10 days or as directed by a healthcare provider
Monitor liver function and adjust dosage as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to treat jaundice, an ailment characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to liver dysfunction. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to treat jaundice by reducing liver inflammation and protecting liver cells from oxidative damage. The bioactive constituents of Artemisia absinthium, including sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects against jaundice by modulating liver function and promoting the elimination of bilirubin. The benefits of using Artemisia absinthium teas to treat jaundice include improved liver function, reduced bilirubin levels, and a decrease in the risk of liver damage and cirrhosis.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Artemisia absinthium leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Remove the pot from heat and add the Artemisia absinthium leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to manage jaundice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat jaundice, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, liver damage, and increased risk of bleeding due to the presence of thujone, a toxic compound. Precautions include avoiding excessive consumption, as it can lead to symptoms like dizziness, nausea, and headaches, and using it under proper preparation and dosing to minimize risks.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Artemisia absinthium tea, 3 times a day.
Start with 2 tablespoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Increase the dose to 3 tablespoons per cup if symptoms persist.
Use Artemisia absinthium tea for 3-5 days to see noticeable improvements.
Monitor liver function and consult a doctor if symptoms worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Cichorium intybus teas have been traditionally used to treat jaundice, an ailment characterized by yellowing of the skin and eyes due to liver dysfunction. The hepato-protective properties of this herbal preparation help to treat jaundice by promoting liver health, improving bile flow, and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents, such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, in Cichorium intybus teas contribute to its therapeutic effects by enhancing liver function and reducing inflammation. The benefits of using Cichorium intybus teas to treat jaundice include improved liver function, reduced bilirubin levels, and alleviation of symptoms such as nausea and fatigue.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Cichorium intybus leaves and flowers. Chop them into small pieces.
Heat 2 cups of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the chopped Cichorium intybus to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day for jaundice relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Cichorium intybus teas for jaundice were found to be one of the most frequent herbs used by neonates and their feeding mother to treat jaundice in Qom, Iran.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cichorium intybus teas can be beneficial in treating jaundice, but some possible side effects include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions, particularly in individuals with sensitive skin or digestive systems. Precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, as this may exacerbate underlying conditions such as kidney or liver problems, and pregnant or breastfeeding women should be cautious when using Cichorium intybus teas due to limited research on its safety in these populations.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Cichorium intybus tea for jaundice treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Cichorium intybus tea 3 times a day for 2 weeks.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Cichorium intybus leaves per cup of boiling water.
Consume 2-3 cups of tea daily, for up to 4 weeks under doctor's guidance.
Monitor liver function and report changes to your doctor regularly.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat jaundice, an ailment characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to liver dysfunction. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, help to improve liver function and reduce bilirubin levels, thus alleviating jaundice symptoms. The tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also aid in protecting the liver from further damage and promoting its natural detoxification process. By consuming Ginkgo biloba tea, individuals can benefit from its hepatoprotective effects, reduced oxidative stress, and improved overall liver health, making it a potential natural remedy for jaundice treatment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of water in a pot.
Heat the water in the pot until it boils.
Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the leaves.
Drink 1/4 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day after consulting a doctor.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat jaundice, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat jaundice, including avoiding consumption in people with bleeding disorders, pregnant women, and those with kidney or liver disease, as it may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor for jaundice diagnosis and Ginkgo biloba tea usage approval.
Take 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea, 2-3 times a day, for 7-10 days.
Start with 1 tablespoon of Ginkgo biloba extract per cup of tea.
Monitor liver function and bilirubin levels during the treatment period.
Discontinue Ginkgo biloba tea if symptoms worsen or no improvement is seen.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaundice due to their carminative and hepatoprotective properties. The active compounds present in peppermint tea, including limonene, menthone, and menthol, help to alleviate jaundice symptoms by improving liver function and reducing bile duct obstruction. By enhancing liver enzyme activity and promoting bile flow, peppermint tea helps to reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate the characteristic yellowing of the skin and eyes associated with jaundice. The benefits of peppermint tea in treating jaundice include rapid relief from symptoms, improved liver health, and a reduced risk of liver damage.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh mint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mint leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool down.
Strain the liquid to remove the mint leaves. Discard the leaves.
Mix 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) with the cooled mint tea to make it more palatable.
Drink 1-2 cups of the mint tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Plants (Basel, Switzerland), Mentha x piperita teas for jaundice are not mentioned in the study, but the study does show that Mentha x piperita has significant amounts of phenolic compounds that are responsible for tyrosinase inhibition activity, which can be beneficial for skin health, but there is no specific mention of its use for jaundice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to treat jaundice, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using these teas to treat jaundice, it is essential to take precautions, such as drinking plenty of water, avoiding overconsumption, and monitoring blood sugar levels, as excessive consumption can lead to hypoglycemia.

preparations.herb_desc: Rheum palmatum teas have been traditionally used to treat jaundice due to their hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery. The bioactive constituents of Rheum palmatum, including rhein, emodin, and anthraquinones, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities that help to protect the liver and reduce bilirubin levels. By consuming Rheum palmatum teas, patients can benefit from its ability to stimulate liver function, improve bile flow, and reduce oxidative stress, ultimately leading to the resolution of jaundice symptoms. The use of Rheum palmatum teas as a complementary treatment for jaundice has shown promising results, highlighting the potential of this herbal preparation in supporting liver health and promoting overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rheum palmatum leaves and 2 cups of water.
Wash the Rheum palmatum leaves with clean water and chop them into small pieces.
Boil the 2 cups of water in a pot and add the chopped Rheum palmatum leaves.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes. Then, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for jaundice relief. You can also store the tea in the refrigerator for later use.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rheum palmatum teas can be used to treat jaundice, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as increased risk of bleeding due to its blood-thinning properties. To use Rheum palmatum teas safely, take precautions to avoid consuming large quantities, as it may exacerbate kidney issues or interact with other medications, and also consider the risk of allergic reactions or interactions with other health conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas, also known as German chamomile, have been traditionally used to treat jaundice in infants due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as apigenin and luteolin, present in Matricaria chamomilla teas, help to reduce inflammation and promote liver function, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms. This herbal preparation helps to treat jaundice by promoting the excretion of bilirubin and improving the overall digestive health, making it an effective natural remedy for this condition. The benefits of using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat jaundice include reduced risk of complications, improved liver function, and a decrease in the severity of jaundice symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers.

Precautions should be taken when using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat jaundice, including avoiding its use in people with liver or kidney problems, pregnant women, and breastfeeding mothers.

preparations.herb_desc: Vitis vinifera teas have been used traditionally to treat jaundice due to their hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties. These herbal preparations help to treat jaundice by promoting liver function, reducing inflammation, and enhancing bile production, thus facilitating the breakdown of bilirubin and its excretion from the body. The bioactive constituents of Vitis vinifera teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, contribute to their therapeutic effects by modulating the activity of liver enzymes, scavenging free radicals, and modulating the immune response. The benefits of using Vitis vinifera teas to treat jaundice include improved liver function, reduced oxidative stress, and a decrease in the severity of symptoms associated with this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of dried Vitis vinifera leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a strainer.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Vitis vinifera leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried Vitis vinifera leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a strainer. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for jaundice relief. Consult a doctor before using this remedy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vitis vinifera teas can be used to treat jaundice, but some possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea, stomach pain, and nausea, as well as allergic reactions like rashes and itching. When using Vitis vinifera teas to treat jaundice, it is essential to note that individuals with liver or kidney problems, as well as pregnant or breastfeeding women, should be cautious and monitor their body's response to the herbal preparation, as it may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Cynara scolymus teas, also known as artichoke tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaundice due to their hepatoprotective and choleretic properties. The tea's ability to stimulate bile production and reduce liver inflammation helps to alleviate jaundice symptoms, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition. The bioactive constituents of Cynara scolymus tea, such as cynarin, luteolin, and quercetin, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that contribute to its therapeutic effects. By promoting liver health and enhancing bile flow, Cynara scolymus teas can help to treat jaundice and improve overall liver function, providing a natural and potentially beneficial treatment option.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Cynara scolymus leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice.
Combine 1 cup of boiling water and 2-3 teaspoons of Cynara scolymus leaves in a cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Mix the steeped tea with honey and lemon juice, and drink 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cynara scolymus teas can be used to treat jaundice, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, as it may interact with other medications and worsen conditions such as kidney or liver disease, so it's essential to monitor liver function and blood pressure closely.

preparations.herb_desc: Arctium lappa teas have been traditionally used to treat jaundice, a condition characterized by a yellowing of the skin and eyes due to liver dysfunction. The diuretic and hepatoprotective properties of Arctium lappa teas help to treat jaundice by promoting the removal of excess bilirubin from the body and protecting the liver from damage. The bioactive constituents of Arctium lappa teas, including inulin, mucilage, and sesquiterpene lactones, help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of liver cells. The benefits of using Arctium lappa teas to treat jaundice include improved liver function, reduced inflammation, and a decrease in bilirubin levels, making it a potentially effective natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Arctium lappa root.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Add the dried root to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day for jaundice relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Arctium lappa teas can be used to treat jaundice, but they may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Arctium lappa teas safely, it is recommended to monitor liver function regularly, start with a low dose, and avoid using it in combination with other medications that can affect liver function, as well as to be cautious when using it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat jaundice, a condition characterized by a yellowing of the skin and eyes due to high bilirubin levels in the blood. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often associated with jaundice. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides, phenolic acids, and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to have hepatoprotective and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to alleviate jaundice symptoms. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat jaundice include reduced liver damage, improved liver function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Measure 1 cup of the flowers and add to a large cup.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the flowers.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for jaundice relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, information about the use of Echinacea purpurea teas for jaundice is not provided, but it was among the commonly used botanical dietary supplements (used by 15.44% of respondents).
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat jaundice, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include monitoring liver function, being cautious with dosage, and avoiding consumption during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to potential liver toxicity.

preparation_study: According to Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, Silybum marianum teas, which contain silymarin, were used as a positive control in the study, showing a protective effect against liver damage, but no specific information about Silybum marianum teas for jaundice was found in the study.
intro_study: According to the study, teas for jaundice may be beneficial, as the study highlights the potential of plant pharmacology and medicinal plants in treating jaundice, including a total of 207 medicinal plants.
